publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 138-139. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: Dearest William. I read the enclosed, with no surprise. It is exactly what I anticipated would some time be made; & shocking as it all is to reflect on, I have so long in my own mind felt the connection that such things, or something very similar, had been, in
my most miserable & unhappy family, that the proofs of the fact come to me as no new or startling thing.--

It is tho' a most strange & dreadful history! And to think that my Mother, {then we may say a young girl [double underlined]), & purity & innocence itself, should have been suddenly plunged into the midst of the greatest depths of depravity & crimes, is almost more dreadful than the crimes themselves. I only wonder she has a particle
of confidence in anybody whatever. I do indeed wonder she has!--
I rather wish you could have seen what I have written to my Mother. But it would lose time to send it to you, & I can repeat most of it when you come.
My different affairs go on well on the whole; but the weather is very gloomy, & I am rather dull than otherwise.--
I have many little things to tell you when you come,
& I think you will approve of the arrangements I contemplate for the dispersal of my various occupations & plans.--
Very glad indeed about Parrate's Son.--
